title: 10 best herbal tinctures for pink eye
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for pink eye are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate the symptoms of conjunctivitis, a common and contagious condition characterized by redness, itchiness, and discharge in the eyes. These natural remedies help by reducing inflammation, combating bacterial or viral infections, and soothing dryness and irritation. Examples of herbal tinctures effective against pink eye include Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Marshmallow root, which can be added to eyewash solutions or applied topically with a cotton ball. This holistic approach improves lives by providing a safe and chemical-free alternative to conventional treatments, reducing the risk of side effects and promoting natural healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, while also combating bacterial and viral infections that can cause the condition. Additionally, pot marigold's antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
Antimicrobial: The tincture's antimicrobial properties can help combat the underlying infection causing pink eye, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster recovery.

Soften and loosen discharge: Pot marigold's mucilages (gum-like substances) can soften and loosen thick or sticky discharge in the eyes, making it easier to flush out and helping to clear up congestion.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, making them effective in soothing and healing pink eye.
Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of pink eye, such as redness and swelling.

Nascent triterpene esters (including isovaleryl angelate and isobutyryl angelate): These esters exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help prevent the spread of pink eye and promote the healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain high levels of bioactive compounds, particularly triterpenoids and carotenoids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for treating pink eye.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain similar bioactive compounds as the flowers, providing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in treating pink eye.

Stems: The stems are used because they also contain bioactive compounds, although to a lesser extent than the flowers and leaves, which can still contribute to the overall antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh calendula flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them immediately in a clean paper bag for 2-3 weeks.
Measure out 1 part of the dried flowers to 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it to allow the flavors to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound, echinacea, soothes redness and swelling in the eyes, reducing discomfort and irritation. Additionally, its antibacterial and antiviral agents help combat the underlying infection, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications. By addressing both the inflammation and infection simultaneously, Kansas coneflower tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for treating pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea pallida's compounds, such as caryophyllene and sesquiterpenes, exhibit antibacterial activity against Haemophilus influenzae, a common cause of Pink Eye, and also reduce inflammation in the conjunctiva, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Immune system modulation: Kansas Coneflower tincture contains bioactive compounds like polysaccharides and flavonoids that can stimulate the immune system, increasing its response to infection and promoting the production of antibodies to fight off Pink Eye-causing pathogens.

Antioxidant activity: Echinacea pallida's antioxidants, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help protect ocular tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions like Pink Eye.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial or viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting a faster recovery.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia may exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and swelling associated with pink eye.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Echinacea angustifolia may stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections and reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of echinacea angustifolia in the fall, after the first frost, for optimal potency and quality.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces, using about 1 part root to 2 parts menstruum by weight.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, using a 1:2 ratio by volume.
Store the jar in a cool dark place, shaking it daily for 2 to 6 weeks, to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter, discarding the solids, and transfer the tincture to dark glass dropper bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with pink eye because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's antimicrobial compounds also help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye. Additionally, eyebright's astringent properties constrict blood vessels and reduce discharge, providing relief from itching and burning sensations. As the tincture is absorbed into the bloodstream, it reaches the affected areas quickly, providing fast and effective relief from pink eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Eyebright's flavonoids and terpenes help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which alleviates discomfort and promotes healing.

Antimicrobial activity: The plant's essential oils, including eucalyptol and menthol, exhibit antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat the underlying cause of pink eye.

Vasoconstrictive and anti-edematous properties: Eyebright's volatile compounds constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup in the eyes, which helps to alleviate redness, itchiness, and swelling associated with pink eye.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes associated with pink eye.
Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid, caffeic acid): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, helping to combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye.
Iridoid glycosides (e.g., euphrasine, echinacoside): These compounds have a soothing effect on the eyes, reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Euphrasia officinalis leaves are considered the most potent and commonly used part for their medicinal properties.
Flowers: Euphrasia officinalis flowers are used to create tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm the eyes.
Stems: Euphrasia officinalis stems are also used to make tinctures, which are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves in the early morning on a dry day.
Clean the harvested material thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the cleaned euphrasia officinalis with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The soothing properties of marshmallow root also calm irritated conjunctiva and provide relief from itchiness and burning sensations. Additionally, the antimicrobial agents present in the tincture can help to combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing the pink eye, promoting faster recovery and healing.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root's mucilage property helps to soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tannins present in marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pink eye.
Antioxidants: Marshmallow root's antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions like conjunctivitis.
remedy_constituents: Galactomannans: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with pink eye.
Mucilages: Mucilages are a type of thick, gel-like substance that can help soothe and protect the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Phenolic acids (including caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial or viral infections that may be causing pink eye, while also reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are the most commonly used part for making tinctures, as they contain the highest concentration of mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tinctures, as they contain similar mucilages to the roots, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in pink eye.

Buds: The buds of Althaea officinalis are occasionally used to make tinctures, as they contain a higher concentration of mucilages and other active compounds than the leaves, making them a more potent remedy for pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100 grams of fresh or dried althaea officinalis roots and leaves in the morning.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids present in licorice root, such as isoliquiritigenin and licochalcone A, have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from redness and itching associated with pink eye. Additionally, the tannins in licorice root have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate redness associated with pink eye.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, protecting the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by pink eye.
Mucilage-forming properties: Licorice root's mucilage helps soothe and lubricate the affected area, providing relief from itchiness, burning, and discharge common in pink eye.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These saponins help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated conjunctiva, which can contribute to the symptoms of pink eye.
Glycyrrhizin: As a potent anti-inflammatory compound, glycyrrhizin can help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making it a potential remedy for pink eye.

Flavonoids (including Isoflavones): Flavonoids have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to combat bacterial or viral infections that cause pink eye and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizic acid, a compound found in the roots, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Roots: The roots also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial or viral infections causing pink eye.
Roots: Glycyrrhizic acid in the roots has a soothing effect, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable supplier or cultivate it in your garden.
Chop the dried root into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped root with 80 percent ethanol in a ratio of 1 part root to 5 parts ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with pink eye because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that cause conjunctivitis. The bioactive compounds in elder tinctures, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have potent antimicrobial activity against common bacteria like Staphylococcus aureus and Streptococcus pneumoniae, helping to soothe and heal pink eye naturally.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help combat the viral infection responsible for pink eye, reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye, promoting healing and relief from symptoms.

Immune system support: Elderberry tincture contains compounds that stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections like pink eye and reducing the risk of complications or reoccurrence.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and combat the viral or bacterial infection causing pink eye.
Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate the discomfort and swelling associated with pink eye.
Vaccarin: This flavonoid has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and potentially reduce the severity and duration of pink eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are rich in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the affected eye.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used in tinctures due to their antiseptic and antiviral properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Buds: The buds of Sambucus nigra contain antiseptic and antibacterial compounds that help to fight off the underlying infection and promote the healing of the affected eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra berries in late summer early fall when they are ripe and red.
Dry the sambucus nigra berries in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep one part of the sambucus nigra powder in two parts of 60 vodka for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the sambucus nigra tincture using a coffee filter and transfer it to glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with pink eye because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat bacterial and viral infections that cause the condition. The tincture's active compound, berberine, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae, two common bacteria associated with pink eye. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discharge, providing fast relief from discomfort and promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antibacterial properties, which helps to combat the infection-causing bacteria Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae that contribute to Pink Eye.
Berberin's anti-inflammatory effects: Reduces swelling and inflammation in the conjunctiva, providing relief from redness, itching, and discharge associated with Pink Eye.
Antioxidant properties: Protects the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate Pink Eye symptoms and potentially lead to long-term eye problems.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to pink eye.

Vasaka (Ipecacuanha alkaloid): Not a main constituent of Hydrastis canadensis, but it's worth mentioning, however, another constituent with similar properties is hydrastine. However, it's the berberine which is the main constituent of Hydrastis canadensis  which may help reduce inflammation and combat infections associated with pink eye.

Alkaloids (including hydrastine): These compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and heal the affected area, reducing redness and discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with antimicrobial properties that helps combat pink eye.
Leaves: They are also used as they contain berberine and other alkaloids that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting infections associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean hydrastis canadensis roots and stems in a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts solvent.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and soak it in a high-proof spirit such as everclear for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the solvent.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to store it.
Allow the tincture to mature for 4 to 6 weeks before using it to ensure optimal potency and quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The mucilage contained in slippery elm acts as a soothing agent, coating and protecting the delicate tissues in the eyes and conjunctiva. This can help to alleviate discomfort and promote healing by reducing inflammation and irritation, allowing the body's natural healing processes to take over and resolve the infection more quickly.

Mucilage-forming: When applied topically to the affected area, slippery elm's mucilages help to create a protective barrier that shields the delicate tissues of the eye from further irritation.
Antioxidant: Slippery elm contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and tissue damage in pink eye cases.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce bacterial or viral infections in the eyes that cause pink eye.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Alkaloids (specifically Berberine): Berberine has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye, and also has anti-inflammatory effects to reduce eye inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh or dried ulmus rubra bark and 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Mix the ulmus rubra bark and vodka together in the glass jar ensuring all plant material is saturated.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and label it for future use with a 1:5 ratio of tincture to water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce redness and swelling. The tannins present in comfrey also help to soothe the affected area, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause infection, promoting a quicker recovery. By using herbal comfrey tinctures, individuals suffering from pink eye can experience relief from symptoms and accelerated healing without relying on harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds in comfrey, including salicylic acid and camphor, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause conjunctivitis (pink eye), promoting healing and reducing infection.

Wound-healing properties: Comfrey's high concentration of allantoin, a natural wound healer, helps stimulate tissue repair and accelerate the recovery process, reducing scarring and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Comfrey tinctures, such as rosmarinic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye.
Phenolics: Phenolic compounds in Comfrey, including quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help soothe and protect the eyes from infection.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe the affected area.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which help to combat pink eye.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to calm the irritated eyes and reduce discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather symphytum officinale roots and stems in a quantity of 250 grams and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of 80% ethanol and steep for 2 weeks.
Shake the mixture thoroughly every day for 14 days to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for 4 weeks before use.

When applied topically as a tincture, the active compounds in plantain work to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with pink eye. remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried plantago major leaves and flowers and combine with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the solvent to extract the plant's active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c or lower to preserve the potency.
Label the bottles with the date the tincture was made and the ingredients used including the 1:2 ratio of plant to solvent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with pink eye because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive compounds in yarrow, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause conjunctivitis, reduce inflammation and swelling, and soothe irritated eyes. Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial agents, including lactones and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause pink eye infections, promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidants help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue damage in cases of pink eye.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning or evening for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop the plant material into small pieces and combine them with a 70% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, squeezing the plant material to extract as much liquid as possible.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place and use within 2 years, administering 20-30 drops 3 times daily as needed for pink eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with pink eye because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tannins present in the tincture help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, while its antimicrobial compounds combat bacterial and fungal infections that often cause pink eye. Additionally, maidenhair tree's antioxidant properties help to soothe the eyes and promote healthy tear production, aiding in the healing process. remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Ginkgo biloba's antibacterial compounds, such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, help combat bacterial infections associated with pink eye, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tinctures have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye, making it a soothing treatment for irritated eyes.

Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, making them effective against bacterial and viral infections causing pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the liquid reaches a dark green color.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the plant material.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, using 20-30 drops twice daily as needed to help alleviate pink eye symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried turmeric roots measuring one cup for this recipe.
Chop the turmeric roots into small pieces and add two cups of vodka.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar at room temperature for six weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Store the final tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest serenoa repens leaves and stems in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Clean and dry the harvested material by air-drying it for several days or by using a food dehydrator at low heat.
Chop the dried material into small pieces to release its essential oils and prepare it for extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped serenoa repens with 5 parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering the tincture to remove any solids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of chamomile can help combat bacterial infections, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae, that may cause or contribute to pink eye, reducing the risk of complications.

Sotheing and soothing: Chamomile's calming effects can help reduce discomfort, irritation, and inflammation in the eyes, promoting a sense of relaxation and easing the symptoms associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory tincture that can help reduce redness and swelling in pink eye.
Flowers: The flowers' calming and antiseptic properties make them an excellent choice for creating a tincture that can help alleviate itchiness and inflammation associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or dusk for optimal potency.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine the chopped flowers with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with pink eye because of its potent antibacterial properties, which effectively combat the bacterial infection that causes the condition. The citral and geraniol compounds present in lemongrass have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of microorganisms, including those responsible for conjunctivitis. Additionally, lemongrass tinctures may also help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, providing relief from redness, itchiness, and discharge associated with pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Lemongrass tincture contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat the underlying infection causing pink eye.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass tincture's flavonoids and terpenes can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from discomfort and redness associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass tincture's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by pink eye, promoting faster recovery and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that help combat bacterial infections, rhyzomes are an excellent choice for treating the bacterial component of pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus at dawn or before sunrise for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase surface area exposure.
Combine 2 cups of chopped cymbopogon citratus with 2 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar allowing them to infuse.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days shaking gently twice daily to facilitate tincture extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and storing the tincture in an amber-colored bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with pink eye because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the affected area. The antibacterial and antiviral compounds found in ginger also work to combat the underlying infection causing the condition. Additionally, ginger's soothing qualities can provide relief from itching and burning sensations, promoting a more comfortable recovery. By using herbal ginger tinctures, individuals may experience faster healing times and reduced discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce swelling and irritation in the conjunctiva and eyelids associated with pink eye.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of ginger tincture, particularly its ability to inhibit bacterial growth, help combat the underlying infection causing pink eye, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Pain-relieving property: Ginger's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate the discomfort, pain, and burning sensation associated with pink eye, making it more manageable for patients.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the primary source of active compounds responsible for anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating pink eye.
Barks: The bark contains bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh ginger rhizomes of 2-3 inches in length and wash them thoroughly with clean water.
Dry the cleaned ginger rhizomes in a single layer on a paper towel for 24 hours.
Grind the dried ginger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 100 grams of ground ginger powder with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, seal tightly, and shake vigorously daily for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids, and store the tincture in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling in the affected area. The cooling sensation of peppermint also provides temporary relief from itching and burning sensations associated with pink eye. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infection that causes pink eye, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications. By soothing and calming the infected eyes, peppermint tinctures can provide quick and effective relief for pink eye sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tincture reduces redness and swelling associated with pink eye due to its potent anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid.
Antiviral properties: The menthol and menthone in peppermint tincture have antiviral activity, helping to combat the viral pathogens that cause pink eye (conjunctivitis) and reduce its severity.
Pain-relieving properties: Peppermint's analgesic compounds, including menthol, provide quick relief from the discomforting itching, burning, and gritty sensation associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots are often used to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of menthol and menthone, peppermint rhyzomes help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier weighing about 250 grams.
Chop the peppermint leaves finely using scissors or a mortar and pestle for approximately 10 minutes.
Combine the chopped peppermint leaves with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar leaving about an inch at the top.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing it to infuse for 2 weeks shaking the mixture daily for 30 seconds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the bottle with the preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with pink eye because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's essential oils, such as citral and limonene, work to reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing fast relief from discomfort and pain. Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in lemon verbena tincture can reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort and promoting recovery.

Buds: The buds of the lemon verbena plant contain high amounts of essential oils, which have antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help combat infections causing pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and stems, with no visible signs of pests or mold.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces, using scissors or sharp pruning shears, to release the oils.
Combine 250 grams of chopped verbena officinalis with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring good coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar twice daily during this period.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pink eye that are treated with herbal tinctures are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and meibomian gland dysfunction. These conditions often involve inflammation or infection of the eyes and eyelids, causing redness, itchiness, and discharge. Herbal tinctures such as goldenseal, echinacea, and eyebright can help to reduce inflammation, fight off bacteria and viruses, and promote healing in these conditions. They may be used alone or in combination with conventional treatments under the guidance of a healthcare practitioner.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with pink eye is a blend of Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Eyebright. Echinacea supports the immune system to fight off infections, while Goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help reduce bacterial growth. Eyebright, rich in antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, soothes and calms the affected area. Take 5-10 drops of each tincture, 2-3 times a day, diluted in water or added to a warm compress to help alleviate symptoms and promote healing. Consult a healthcare professional for guidance.
